tests.name: Real vs Fake Section (302 vs 999)
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.927 similarity
tests.text_a: Section 302 IPC deals with punishment for murder.
tests.text_b: Section 999 IPC deals with punishment for murder.

tests.name: Real vs Non-existent High Number
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.874 similarity
tests.text_a: Section 376 IPC - punishment for rape.
tests.text_b: Section 9999 IPC - punishment for rape.

tests.name: Two Different Real Sections
tests.reason: ✅ Good separation: 0.569
tests.text_a: Section 302 IPC (murder) punishment is death or life imprisonment.
tests.text_b: Section 304 IPC (culpable homicide) punishment is 10 years or life.

tests.name: Real vs Real (Similar Domains)
tests.reason: ✅ Good separation: 0.077
tests.text_a: Section 376 IPC - rape, minimum 7 years.
tests.text_b: Section 354 IPC - assault, up to 2 years.

tests.name: Guilty vs Not Guilty
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.997 similarity
tests.text_a: The accused is guilty of murder under Section 302.
tests.text_b: The accused is NOT guilty of murder under Section 302.

tests.name: Convicted vs Acquitted
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.991 similarity
tests.text_a: The court convicted the defendant for theft.
tests.text_b: The court acquitted the defendant of theft.

tests.name: With vs Without Consent
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.996 similarity
tests.text_a: The act was done with consent of the victim.
tests.text_b: The act was done without consent of the victim.

tests.name: Sanctioned vs Not Sanctioned
tests.reason: ⚠️ Borderline: 0.843 similarity (expected <0.75)
tests.text_a: The government sanctioned the project.
tests.text_b: The government did NOT sanction the project.

tests.name: Maharashtra vs Karnataka Law
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.978 similarity
tests.text_a: The Maharashtra Rent Control Act applies to properties in Mumbai.
tests.text_b: The Karnataka Rent Control Act applies to properties in Bangalore.

tests.name: Supreme Court vs High Court
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.985 similarity
tests.text_a: The Supreme Court of India has appellate jurisdiction.
tests.text_b: The Delhi High Court has appellate jurisdiction.

tests.name: Civil vs Criminal Court
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.988 similarity
tests.text_a: The civil court has jurisdiction over property disputes.
tests.text_b: The criminal court has jurisdiction over theft cases.

tests.name: State vs Central Law
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.971 similarity
tests.text_a: Under the Uttar Pradesh Prohibition Act.
tests.text_b: Under the Central Prohibition Act.

tests.name: IPC (1860) vs BNS (2023) - Murder
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.897 similarity
tests.text_a: Section 302 IPC: Punishment for murder is death or life imprisonment.
tests.text_b: Section 101 BNS: Punishment for murder is death or life imprisonment.

tests.name: IPC vs BNS - Theft
tests.reason: ✅ Good separation: 0.434
tests.text_a: Section 378 IPC defines theft.
tests.text_b: Section 303 BNS defines theft.

tests.name: Companies Act 1956 vs 2013
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.946 similarity
tests.text_a: Section 271 of Companies Act 1956 deals with winding up.
tests.text_b: Section 271 of Companies Act 2013 deals with winding up.

tests.name: IT Act 2000 vs DPDP Act 2023
tests.reason: ⚠️ Borderline: 0.814 similarity (expected <0.75)
tests.text_a: Section 43 of IT Act 2000 deals with data protection.
tests.text_b: Section 8 of DPDP Act 2023 deals with data protection.

tests.name: Fake Law vs Real Law
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.991 similarity
tests.text_a: Under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
tests.text_b: Under the Information Technology Act, 2000.

tests.name: Non-legal vs Legal Text
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.961 similarity
tests.text_a: The stock market crashed by 5% today.
tests.text_b: The court dismissed the petition due to lack of evidence.

tests.name: Random vs Legal Concept
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.970 similarity
tests.text_a: Quantum physics explains the behavior of particles.
tests.text_b: Quantum meruit is a legal doctrine for unjust enrichment.

tests.name: Fictional vs Real Case
tests.reason: ✅ Good separation: 0.090
tests.text_a: The case of Kumar vs State of India (2050).
tests.text_b: The case of Kesavananda Bharati vs State of Kerala (1973).

tests.name: CPC vs CrPC
tests.reason: ✅ Good separation: 0.683
tests.text_a: Order 39 CPC deals with temporary injunctions.
tests.text_b: Section 144 CrPC deals with temporary orders.

tests.name: Civil Suit vs Criminal Case
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.969 similarity
tests.text_a: The plaintiff filed a suit for recovery of money.
tests.text_b: The state filed a case for theft under IPC.

tests.name: Tort vs Crime
tests.reason: ✅ Good separation: -0.017
tests.text_a: The defendant committed negligence causing injury.
tests.text_b: The accused committed assault causing injury.

tests.name: Remedies: Damages vs Imprisonment
tests.reason: Should be different but got 0.976 similarity
tests.text_a: The court awarded monetary damages to the plaintiff.
tests.text_b: The court sentenced the accused to 5 years imprisonment.
